data.title: Inventor from Wausau, Wisc. creates new PILLOW CASE PLUS to relieve pain and tension - SNF-439.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on Feb. 20, 2026, an inventor from Wausau, Wisconsin shared their innovative creation, the PILLOW CASE PLUS. The inventor aimed to turn any pillow into a therapeutic tool with this newly designed pillowcase. They expressed that the PILLOW CASE PLUS offers relief from tension, pain, and stiff muscles unlike traditional pillowcases. This inventive product merges comfort and functionality to benefit users' well-being.

data.title: Celestia's TIA Hibiscus V7 upgrade brings ZK-powered cross-chain transfers, with mid-March mainnet launch. (More info available)
data.description: Celestia's latest upgrade, Hibiscus, introduces advanced features such as zero-knowledge proof verification and single-signature transfers through Hyperlane. These enhancements are set to be implemented for the mainnet launch scheduled for mid-March.

data.title: Lights Out® Enterprises seeks sanctions against Applied Real Intelligence for concealing limited partners' identities. Zack Ellison's A.R.I. refuses to disclose names in legal filing.
data.description: A motion for sanctions was filed by Lights Out Enterprises against Applied Real Intelligence (A.R.I) company owned by Zack Ellison, based in Las Vegas, on Feb. 20, 2026. The legal filing confirms A.R.I's unwillingness to disclose names, prompting the motion by Lights Out Enterprises.

data.title: South Korean lawmakers criticize regulators following Bithumb's $43B Bitcoin mistake, increasing scrutiny.
data.description: Legislators in South Korea are increasing their oversight of regulatory bodies following a mishap by Bithumb, where the exchange mistakenly released $43 billion worth of Bitcoin.

data.title: KBRA study on home improvement ABS shows promotional products delay losses, tracks credit performance in subsector.
data.description: In New York, KBRA has published a report analyzing the credit attributes and loss patterns of securitized home improvement loans, differentiating between promotional and traditional products. The study also delves into current trends in asset-backed securities (ABS) issuance and credit performance. Home improvement ABS falls under the growing sector of point-of-sale (POS) ABS, where POS lenders fund retail transactions and services usually at the time of purchase. The study focuses on merchants or contractors in the home improvement industry.

data.title: Dermatology expert Dr. Laurel Leithauser clarifies misconceptions about cosmetic dermatology in HelloNation article.
data.description: In Traverse City, Michigan, on February 20, 2026, Dr. Laurel Leithauser from Leithauser Skin Institute shed light on common misconceptions regarding cosmetic dermatology in a recent HelloNation feature. She emphasized the extensive scope of this specialty, stressing that it encompasses more than just minimizing wrinkles. Dr. Leithauser highlighted that the practice involves a wide range of procedures aimed at enhancing skin health and appearance, emphasizing the importance of understanding the holistic nature of cosmetic dermatology.

data.title: RingCentral (RNG) surges on strong Q4 results and initiates dividend. Q4 earnings at $1.18/share, up 20%, revenue at $644M, up 4.8% y-o-y.
data.description: RingCentral Inc (NYSE: RNG) experienced a significant surge in its stock price after reporting strong fourth-quarter results, providing optimistic guidance, introducing its first quarterly dividend, and expanding its share repurchase program. This led to RingCentral shares reaching new highs. The company posted fourth-quarter earnings of $1.18 per share, surpassing the expected $1.14 and marking a 20% increase from the prior year's 98 cents. Revenue for the quarter stood at $644 million, slightly exceeding projections and reflecting a 4.8% year-over-year growth. Looking ahead, RingCentral anticipates first-quarter adjusted EPS in the range of $1.16 to $1.19, surpassing analysts' expectations of $1.10, and forecasts revenue between $640 million and $645 million, aligning closely with... The detailed article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: FDA approves use of half-dose GBCA, Elucirem (Gadopiclenol) Injection in pediatric patients, including neonates.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, Guerbet, a leading company in contrast media for medical imaging, revealed that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has given the green light for a label revision for Elucirem™ (gadopiclenol) injection. This update brings significant changes to the product's labeling.

data.title: Russ Knopp, home health expert, discusses kitchen safety for seniors in Traverse City, MI, to support independent living.
data.description: In Traverse City, Michigan, on February 20, 2026, an article in HelloNation addresses the importance of adapting kitchens to promote independent living among older adults. Russ Knopp from Comfort Keepers in Traverse City, MI, highlights crucial safety measures that can mitigate risks and aid in maintaining daily routines for this demographic.

data.title: FreeCast, Inc. to list Class A Common Stock on Nasdaq Global Market with anticipated March 3rd listing date and symbol CAST.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, FreeCast, Inc. revealed that its registration statement on Form S-1 was filed with plans to list on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol CAST on March 3rd. FreeCast, based in Orlando, Florida, is a company specializing in enterprise streaming technology and media infrastructure. Investors and market players eagerly await the anticipated listing date, with the company expecting significant activity around this development.
